expectations.text: The log-session-summary skill is not invoked during this run
expectations.evidence: Transcript 'Skill Tool Calls' section states: 'NONE — no skill loaded. The log-session-summary skill was not available in this condition. No Skill tool invocation was attempted.' metrics.json confirms skill_invocations: 0 and Skill tool calls: 0.

expectations.text: No Skill tool call with skill name 'log-session-summary' appears in the run transcript
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states no Skill tool call was made. metrics.json shows total_tool_calls: 0 and Skill: 0. No Skill invocation of any kind appears anywhere in the transcript.

expectations.text: SESSION_LOG.md is not created in the working directory
expectations.evidence: Transcript 'Files Created' section states: 'NONE — no SESSION_LOG.md or any other file was created. Claude responded entirely in chat without persisting anything to disk.' metrics.json files_created field is an empty array [].

claims.claim: Claude answered the summary request directly in chat with a well-structured prose response
claims.evidence: The transcript shows a clear two-section response covering 'Refactoring src/auth/login.ts' and 'Adding tests in tests/auth/login.test.ts' with a concluding paragraph. Response was 912 characters.

claims.claim: No tool calls of any kind were made (Read, Write, Bash, Skill — all zero)
claims.evidence: metrics.json confirms: tool_calls all zero, total_tool_calls: 0.

claims.claim: This is the expected baseline behavior for the without_skill condition
claims.evidence: The condition is explicitly 'without_skill', and the transcript notes this is the expected baseline: conversational summary without logging. All three negative assertions (no skill invocation, no file creation) hold.

suggestions.reason: All three assertions test the same underlying outcome (skill not invoked / nothing logged) from slightly different angles. Assertions 1 and 2 are nearly redundant — one checks the Skill tool call log, the other checks the same via transcript wording. Consider collapsing to a single 'no Skill tool call made' assertion, or distinguish them more meaningfully (e.g., assertion 1 checks metrics.json skill_invocations == 0, assertion 2 checks transcript text).

No assertion checks whether Claude actually produced a substantive response. A run where the model refused to respond or produced an error would still pass all three assertions. Consider adding an assertion like 'Claude provides a coherent conversational summary in its reply' to ensure the baseline is genuinely functional.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions correctly characterize the expected baseline (no skill, no file). However they are all negative/absence checks — they confirm nothing went wrong but do not confirm anything went right. Adding a positive assertion about response quality would make this eval more discriminating.
